bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My top values across all issues give me the highest possible utility: issueA=valueF (1.0), issueB=valueF (1.0), issueC=valueF (1.0), issueD=valueF (1.0). This is the natural opening position — anchor high and concede gradually as time progresses and I learn the opponent's preferences.

The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA), which gives me very low utility (0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 = 0.20). We're very early (t=0.02), so I should stay close to my top utility bid. My opening was all valueF (utility = 1.0). I'll make a very minor concession on issueA (my least important issue at 0.1 weight) by dropping to valueE, keeping everything else at valueF to signal I'm willing to negotiate but not capitulate early.

The opponent is stubbornly offering all valueA (minimum utility for me) and hasn't moved at all after two rounds. It's very early (t=0.04), so I have no pressure to concede significantly. I'll stay near my top utility while making a small concession on issueA (low weight for me) to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll hold firm on issueC and issueD (highest weights) and make a minor concession on issueB.

The opponent is stubbornly offering near-minimum values (all valueA) with only a tiny concession on issueC (valueB at t=0.06). It's very early (t=0.06), so I should stay firm on my high-weight issues (issueC and issueD, both 0.3 weight) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. I'll hold issueC and issueD at valueF, concede slightly on issueB to valueE, and drop issueA to valueC (low weight, minor cost). My utility: 0.1*0.41 + 0.2*0.72 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.041 + 0.144 + 0.3 + 0.3 = 0.785, still very high.

The opponent is barely conceding - they've only moved issueC from valueA to valueB, while keeping everything else at valueA. This is very early in the negotiation (t=0.08), so I don't need to make large concessions yet. My highest-weight issues are issueC and issueD (0.3 each), so I should hold firm on those. IssueA has the lowest weight (0.1), so I can concede there. I'll keep issueC and issueD at valueF, slightly concede on issueB, and give more on issueA to signal willingness to negotiate while protecting my high-value issues.

The opponent is very slowly conceding - they've moved issueC from valueA to valueB to valueD, but are holding firm on issueA (valueA), issueB (valueA), and issueD (valueA). Their best offer to me so far gives utility: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.79 + 0.3*0.20 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.237 + 0.06 = 0.357. We're still early (t=0.10), so I should continue making reasonable offers while holding firm on my high-weight issues (issueC and issueD). I'll concede a bit more on issueA (low weight) and keep issueB at valueE, while maintaining issueC and issueD at valueF to protect my highest-utility issues.

The opponent is making very slow concessions - they've only moved issueB from valueA to valueC and issueC from valueA to valueD, while keeping issueA and issueD at valueA. Their offers give me very low utility (around 0.2-0.3). We're still early (t=0.12), so I shouldn't concede too much. My highest-weight issues are issueC and issueD (0.3 each), so I should hold firm on those. The opponent seems to care about issueA and issueD staying at valueA. I'll concede slightly on issueA (low weight for me) and issueB, but keep issueC and issueD at high values. I'll try issueA: valueA, issueB: valueD, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueF to show some movement while protecting my key issues.

The opponent is making very slow concessions - they've only moved issueC from valueA to valueD and back, and issueD from valueA to valueB, while keeping issueA and issueB at valueA. Their offers give me very low utility (around 0.2-0.3). We're still early (t=0.14), so I don't need to concede heavily yet. My highest-weight issues are issueD and issueC (0.3 each). I should hold firm on issueD (valueF) and issueC (high values), while being more flexible on issueA (low weight). My last bid was {A:valueA, B:valueD, C:valueC, D:valueF} with utility ≈ 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.51 + 0.3*0.93 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.02 + 0.102 + 0.279 + 0.3 = 0.701. I'll try a slight concession on issueB while keeping issueC and issueD strong.

The opponent seems to be making very slow concessions - they've moved issueC from valueA to valueD and issueD from valueA to valueD, but are holding firm on issueA and issueB at valueA. Their utility to me is still very low. We're only at t=0.16, so I have time to hold firm on my high-importance issues (issueC and issueD). I'll concede slightly on issueA (low importance) and keep issueD and issueC high, while making a small concession on issueB to signal willingness to negotiate. My current bid at t=0.14 was {A:valueA, B:valueC, C:valueD, D:valueF} with utility ≈ 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.5 + 0.3*0.79 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.02+0.10+0.237+0.30 = 0.657. I'll try to keep utility high while making a small concession on issueD to valueE (still 0.95 utility for that issue).

The opponent seems to be slowly conceding on some issues (issueC moved from valueA to valueB, issueB moved to valueC occasionally, issueD moved to valueD at t=0.16), but keeps reverting. Their latest offer at t=0.18 has issueB: valueC, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA - utility to me is low. I'm still early in the negotiation (t=0.18), so I can hold relatively firm. My highest-weight issues are issueC and issueD (0.3 each). I should concede on issueA (low weight, 0.1) and issueB (0.2) while holding firm on issueC and issueD. I'll try offering issueC: valueD and issueD: valueE to signal willingness to move on issueD slightly, while keeping issueC high.

The opponent is very stubborn - they keep offering valueA for most issues, with only slight movement on issueC (up to valueD once) and issueD (up to valueE). Their best offer to me was at t=0.20: issueA:valueA, issueB:valueA, issueC:valueA, issueD:valueE, which gives me utility of 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.3*0.95 = 0.02+0.04+0.06+0.285 = 0.405. We're only at t=0.20, so I still have time to push for better. My current bid at t=0.18 gives me: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.53 + 0.3*0.79 + 0.3*0.95 = 0.02+0.106+0.237+0.285 = 0.648. I'll make a small concession on issueB (drop to valueB or valueC) while keeping issueC at valueD and issueD at valueE, to signal willingness to move while maintaining good utility.

The opponent seems to be slowly conceding on issueC (moving from valueA to valueD) and issueD (moving from valueA to valueC/E), but holding firm on issueA (always valueA) and issueB (mostly valueA/C). Their latest offer gives me utility: issueA=0.1*0.20 + issueB=0.2*0.20 + issueC=0.3*0.79 + issueD=0.3*0.60 = 0.02+0.04+0.237+0.18 = 0.477. I should continue conceding on low-weight issues (issueA, issueB) while holding firm on high-weight issues (issueC, issueD). I'll try issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueE which gives me: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.53 + 0.3*0.93 + 0.3*0.95 = 0.02+0.106+0.279+0.285 = 0.69. This is a slight concession from my last bid while still maintaining good utility on the important issues.

The opponent seems to be slowly conceding on issueC (moving from valueA to valueD occasionally) and issueD (showing valueC, valueD, valueE at times), but keeps returning to low values. Their latest offer has issueB: valueC, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueC. I'm at t=0.24, still early-mid game, so I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueC and issueD) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. My last bid was {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueE} with utility ≈ 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.53 + 0.3*0.93 + 0.3*0.95 = 0.02 + 0.106 + 0.279 + 0.285 = 0.69. I'll try to slightly adjust to signal flexibility while keeping high utility - perhaps conceding a bit on issueD to valueD while keeping issueC at valueC.

The opponent has been very stubborn, mostly offering valueA across all issues, but I can see some movement: issueC has gone up to valueC, issueD has reached valueD, and issueB has shown valueC occasionally. Their latest offer gives me utility: issueA(0.1×0.20) + issueB(0.2×0.93) + issueC(0.3×0.93) + issueD(0.3×0.80) = 0.02 + 0.186 + 0.279 + 0.24 = 0.725. I should hold firm on issueD (high weight, currently at valueE=0.95) and issueC (high weight, currently at valueC=0.93), while being flexible on issueA (low weight). I'll keep issueD at valueE and issueC at valueC, and try valueC for issueB to signal willingness to meet them there, since we're still early (t=0.26).

The opponent has been mostly stuck at low values (valueA) for most issues, but their latest offer at t=0.28 shows some movement: issueA: valueB, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA. Their utility to me is: 0.1*0.28 + 0.2*0.53 + 0.3*0.68 + 0.3*0.20 = 0.028 + 0.106 + 0.204 + 0.06 = 0.398. We're still early (t=0.28), so I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueC and issueD) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. I'll try to find a bid that might appeal more to the opponent by conceding slightly on issueB (which I care less about) while keeping issueC and issueD high.

The opponent is still making very low offers (mostly valueA across issues), showing minimal concession. Their latest offer at t=0.30 has issueC: valueE and issueD: valueC, which is slightly better. My highest-weight issues are issueD (0.3) and issueC (0.3), so I should hold firm on those. The opponent seems to care about issueA and issueB being low, so I can concede those (low weight for me). I'll maintain issueD: valueE and issueC: valueC while slightly adjusting issueB to show some movement, targeting around 0.75+ utility for myself.

The opponent is very slowly conceding - their latest offer (t=0.32) has issueC: valueD (utility 0.79 for me) and issueD: valueB (utility 0.51 for me), which is better than their usual valueA offers. My current bid at t=0.30 gives me utility: issueA(0.20*0.1) + issueB(0.53*0.2) + issueC(0.79*0.3) + issueD(0.95*0.3) = 0.02 + 0.106 + 0.237 + 0.285 = 0.648. I'll keep issueD at valueE (my highest weight issue) and issueC at valueD, while slightly conceding on issueB.

The opponent's latest offer (t=0.34) is {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueB}, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.50 + 0.3*0.79 + 0.3*0.51 = 0.02 + 0.10 + 0.237 + 0.153 = 0.51.
